headline: Target Outlines Strategic Plan for a New Chapter of Growth in 2026 and Beyond
summary: At its financial community meeting today, Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT) unveiled its multi-year strategy under Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke to accelerate its return to growth. The strategy includes plans to refresh the store experience across the chain, invest in store payroll and training to elevate the guest experience and strengthen key areas of the assortment.

headline: Target sets 'high' bar for 2026 despite continued declining sales

Mizuho senior analyst David Bellinger joins Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman to discuss Target's strategic outlook, leadership under new CEO Fiddelke, and more. headline: Target has another quarter of declining sales but there are signs of improvement to start 2026
summary: Target reported another quarter of declining sales and profits as the retailer struggles to regain its footing with its customers contending with higher prices almost everywhere.  Target also said comparable-store sales rose to start the current quarter.  The company earned $2.30 per share, or $1.05 billion, for the three-month period ended Jan. 31.

headline: Target (NYSE:TGT) Reports Q4 CY2025 In Line With Expectations
summary: General merchandise retailer Target (NYSE:TGT) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025, but sales fell by 1.5% year on year to $30.45 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.44 per share was 13% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

raw.headline: Target (NYSE:TGT) Reports Q4 CY2025 In Line With Expectations

headline: Target: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The results surpassed Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of 13 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $2.17 per share. The retailer posted revenue of $30.45 billion in the period, which did not meet Street forecasts.

raw.headline: Target: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
raw.summary: The results surpassed Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of 13 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $2.17 per share. The retailer posted revenue of $30.45 billion in the period, which did not meet Street forecasts.

headline: Target Sales Just Fell Again. Now Its New CEO Plans for Turnaround.
summary: Target’s  new chief executive,  Michael Fiddelke,  says he has a plan to pull the retailer out of its prolonged funk.  Target reported another sluggish quarter of sales Tuesday, its 13th consecutive quarter of weak or falling sales.  “Our team is firmly focused on writing Target’s next chapter of growth,” by improving merchandising, the shopping experience and technology, said Fiddelke in a statement Tuesday.

raw.headline: Target Sales Just Fell Again. Now Its New CEO Plans for Turnaround.

raw.headline: Target’s Roller Rabbit Deal And Cereal Shift Shape Investor Focus
raw.summary: Target (NYSE:TGT) introduced an exclusive, limited-time collaboration with Roller Rabbit, featuring more than 250 products across new categories such as luggage and outdoor items. The retailer is also moving to remove cereals with certified synthetic colors from its assortment as part of a broader push toward health-focused offerings. These announcements highlight new in-store experiences tied to the Roller Rabbit launch and a shift in grocery choices that aligns with changing consumer...

headline: Target says it's on track to end its sales slump after another lackluster quarter
summary: The big-box retailer Target posted its fiscal fourth-quarter results as it hosted an investor meeting at its Minneapolis headquarters.

headline: Target & CrowdStrike earnings, Fed commentary: What to Watch
summary: Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Tuesday, Mar. 2. More earnings are on deck, with retail names like Target (TGT), Best Buy (BBY), and Ross (ROST) reporting. On Holding (ONON) and CrowdStrike (CRWD) will also report results. Markets will hear commentary from Federal Reserve officials, including New York Fed President John Williams, Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid, and Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari. We will also be keeping an eye on further Iran war developments and oil's (CL=F, BZ=F) price action. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.
